[2018-02-28 23:52] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): but Base set has only Drones, and only some in the rest of available cards.

[2018-02-28 23:54] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): But not Energy spendage.

[2018-02-28 23:55] nivvination: that's completely on purpose

[2018-02-28 23:56] nivvination: no I worded that wrong

[2018-03-01 00:03] wilson0862 (Wilson): Again, that's a design philosophy decision. There's nothing wrong with that.
[2018-03-01 00:03] http404error: One valid complaint to make is that there's a psychological downside of feeling like you're wasting energy, which is one reason energy-using units are popular.
[2018-03-01 00:04] awaclus: I never feel like I'm wasting energy
[2018-03-01 00:04] http404error: That was a roundabout way of saying that.
[2018-03-01 00:04] http404error: You get used to it by playing Prismata, of course.
[2018-03-01 00:04] Deleted User (Deleted User): I think a good way of thinking about energy is econ enabling
[2018-03-01 00:04] http404error: But it's something you have to learn.
[2018-03-01 00:05] awaclus: I mean, I don't think I ever felt like that in the first place
[2018-03-01 00:05] awaclus: I just thought I need engineers to buy drones and when I already had enough drones, I didn't care about engineers
[2018-03-01 00:05] wilson0862 (Wilson): You are pretty quickly socialized in game to understand energy is a "lesser" resource.
[2018-03-01 00:06] http404error: A new player isn't going to automatically know to stop building drones though, that has to be learned as part of the same lesson. That's why it's a nontrivial decision to not institutionalize a non-Drone energy sink.
[2018-03-01 00:06] awaclus: although I also thought that getting a third engineer was some super pro move that ordinary Prismata players wouldn't do

[2018-03-01 00:08] wilson0862 (Wilson): Learning to stop overdroning is part of the learning curve.

[2018-03-01 00:09] wilson0862 (Wilson): New players make bad plays. That's just by definition.

[2018-03-01 01:04] awoodokehu (dokawehu): He's planning on pushing an Exhaust unit kit
[2018-03-01 01:04] awoodokehu (dokawehu): Soon there'll be Exhaust 0 to take advantage of it
[2018-03-01 01:06] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): No, it's more natural to say Exhaust 1.
[2018-03-01 01:06] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Since it can't act for 1 turn.
[2018-03-01 01:12] wilson0862 (Wilson): More natural to you.
[2018-03-01 01:14] jamesa7171: the current exhaust counting system makes the numbers sync up with buildtime numbers (a unit with buildtime 2 and a unit with exhaust 2 will both be usable on the same turn)
[2018-03-01 01:15] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Exhaust 2, you'd expect having 2 turns of disable.
[2018-03-01 01:15] wilson0862 (Wilson): That's just empirically true?
[2018-03-01 01:15] wilson0862 (Wilson): It feels like you have a habit of assuming your subjective preferences/thoughts are universal.
[2018-03-01 01:16] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): You think the other way makes more sense?
[2018-03-01 01:16] wilson0862 (Wilson): I personally think of it like exhaust, you can use it again in this many turns.
[2018-03-01 01:16] wilson0862 (Wilson): So 1 just means next turn.
[2018-03-01 01:17] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): then it wouldn't start from 1, which is odd.
[2018-03-01 01:18] jamesa7171: decreasing all the numbers by 1 means a unit that you can use again next turn would show exhaust 0, which seems awkward
[2018-03-01 01:18] wilson0862 (Wilson): I can see both sides and I'm not going to assume everyone shares my viewpoint. This is kind of getting frustrating though.
[2018-03-01 01:18] wilson0862 (Wilson): Well exhaust 0 just would have an exhaust counter.
[2018-03-01 01:18] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): no, majority fallacy.
[2018-03-01 01:18] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): not my opinion fallacy.
[2018-03-01 01:18] nivvination: ok, so here's the root of it: using a unit "exhausts" it
[2018-03-01 01:19] wilson0862 (Wilson): Literally nomenclature is a majority thing. What even.
[2018-03-01 01:19] nivvination: the game just doesnt say "exhaust 1" on every unit
[2018-03-01 01:19] wilson0862 (Wilson): The purpose of language is to be understood.
[2018-03-01 01:19] wilson0862 (Wilson): So by definition majority matters.
[2018-03-01 01:19] nivvination: language is also subjective af
[2018-03-01 01:20] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): majority being that it's written like that, so people grew accustomed to it.
[2018-03-01 01:20] wilson0862 (Wilson): Yes but irrelevant because you cannot argue with the game on how the keyword should be interpreted.
[2018-03-01 01:20] nivvination: so lets change it because you think everyone should reaccustom themselves to your way of seeing it
[2018-03-01 01:21] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): not for being intuitive.
[2018-03-01 01:21] nivvination: honestly exhaust 2 makes a lot of sense in game
[2018-03-01 01:21] wilson0862 (Wilson): Imagine that. Not everyone intuits the way you do.
[2018-03-01 01:21] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): you're supporting an incorrect position.
[2018-03-01 01:22] wilson0862 (Wilson): Because your intuition disagrees?
[2018-03-01 01:22] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): There's no point in having to substract 1.
[2018-03-01 01:23] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Just because you think it's best.
[2018-03-01 01:23] wilson0862 (Wilson): The irony in that statement is off the charts.
[2018-03-01 01:24] wilson0862 (Wilson): Have a great evening. ❤
[2018-03-01 01:24] wilson0862 (Wilson): Or whatever fits your timezone.
[2018-03-01 01:24] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): you assume me|you are wrong.
[2018-03-01 01:25] nivvination: I love how many different kinds of people there are in the world
[2018-03-01 01:25] nivvination: I also hate how many different kinds of people there are in the world
[2018-03-01 01:26] awoodokehu (dokawehu): okay this will seem out of context to everyone here (but spiritfryer, who's part of it too) but this community has honestly been way more harsh than the ceo one
[2018-03-01 01:30] steel0229e (Steel): hope that's not your impression
[2018-03-01 01:31] Deleted User (Deleted User): I think exhaust 1 is more intuitive, however when u think it through exhaust 2 has to be better
[2018-03-01 01:31] Deleted User (Deleted User): because let's assume a regular unit
[2018-03-01 01:31] Deleted User (Deleted User): if you assume it's exhuast 0, it can play infinite times a turn
[2018-03-01 01:32] steel0229e (Steel): build time and exhaust are both a bit unintuitive but if you get used to it it starts to make more sense than anything else
[2018-03-01 01:32] Deleted User (Deleted User): thus, you have to assume it's exhuast 1
[2018-03-01 01:32] Deleted User (Deleted User): so a delay has to be exhaust 2

[2018-03-01 02:09] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Defense is one of three phases of Prismata gameplay. The other phases are the Breach Phase and Action Phase (which is composed of the sub-phases Purchasing Units and Using Abilities)

[2018-03-01 02:10] http404error: I don't know why you'd think otherwise

[2018-03-01 02:11] mrguy888: Everything on 1 turn is simultaneous.
[2018-03-01 02:11] mrguy888: That is easy to see in a Breachproof mirror.

[2018-03-01 02:12] mrguy888: No but the timers don't tick down until after you defend.
[2018-03-01 02:13] mrguy888: At the start of your turn all the timers go down by 1. When the timer hits 0 you can use the unit.
[2018-03-01 02:13] mrguy888: That seems as intuitive as it can possibly be.

[2018-03-01 02:16] mrguy888: That fundamentally changes something important
[2018-03-01 02:17] mrguy888: There are defenders with build time 1. They would suddenly be prompt. You can't make them build 2 either because they can also attack.
[2018-03-01 02:18] mrguy888: Shredder and Hannibull specifically don't work with those timers without changing how the game works.

[2018-03-01 02:21] ryanfourpm: ie. this unit can't defend on the turn it is built
